title: service-accounts — authorization policies
description: Only ONE policy exists: `ServiceAccountPolicy`. Every ability is gated behind the `platform_admin` guard — the tenant-audience `sanctum` guard NEVER interacts with SA rows directly. Tenant callers reach the SA substrate ONLY when their own tenant's SAs (webhook signers, etc.) are provisioned on their behalf by a platform_admin — the tenant never sees the SA row itself. The token-exchange endpoint is unauthenticated and does NOT flow through this policy.

abilities.scope: Platform-admin surface. Redacted list (no secret material, no last_used_ip).
Show one SA. Includes signer_kid + expiry + status + last_used_at. NEVER the secret_hash.
Audit-usage report. Includes usage histogram, distinct IP-/24 prefixes, jti-reuse detections, last 100 audit rows.
Metadata JSONB (IP allowlist, deployment hints). Separate ability because metadata may carry integration secrets that are inappropriate to expose to ops-baseline roles.
Provision a new SA. Requires --reason. Returns the plaintext secret ONCE.
Update mutable fields. application_id / tenant_id / signer_kid remain immutable regardless of this permission.
Generate + persist a fresh secret. Returns plaintext ONCE.
Set is_enabled=false. Existing JWTs remain valid until natural expiry — hard revocation requires kid burn via the auth module.
Set is_enabled=true. Refuses when expires_at has elapsed.
Soft-delete + disable. Starts the retention hold clock. Actual hard-delete via PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob.
Issue a test JWT for troubleshooting. TTL bounded by min/max config. Audit-logged with trigger=test.

boundary_note: This policy applies EXCLUSIVELY to the `platform_admin` guard. Under the `sanctum` guard, the ServiceAccount row is never a first-class resource — tenant callers cannot list, show, or mutate SAs. Any tenant-audience code that receives an authorization refusal here has taken a wrong path; SA management belongs to the platform plane. The unauthenticated token-exchange endpoint (`POST /api/v1/service-accounts/token`) does NOT flow through this policy — authentication is by secret-hash verification alone, and enrolment safety is enforced by rate limiting + generic error responses.
